task:
# Role & Task
You are an expert landing page designer and conversion-focused copywriter.
Generate a complete, production-quality landing page following the section blueprint below.
Every section must serve a clear conversion purpose. Output ONLY the code files, no explanation.
sectionBlueprint:
# Section Blueprint
Build the landing page with these sections in order. Each section is critical — do not skip any.

## 1. Hero
- **Headline:** One punchy sentence (8–12 words) that states the core value proposition
- **Subheadline:** 1–2 sentences expanding on the headline with specifics
- **CTA Button:** Action-oriented, first-person (e.g., "Start Building Now", "Get My Free Trial")
- **Visual:** Hero image/illustration area or gradient background with subtle animation

## 2. Problem / Pain
- **Headline:** Name the frustration or gap the audience feels
- **Body:** 2–3 short paragraphs or bullet points describing the status quo pain
- Keep it relatable — use "you" language

## 3. Solution Overview
- **Headline:** Introduce the product as the answer
- **Body:** 1 paragraph bridging from the problem to how this product solves it
- Optional: short demo screenshot or code snippet placeholder

## 4. How It Works
- **3-step or 4-step process** with icons/numbers
- Each step: short title + 1-sentence description
- Keep it dead simple — reduce cognitive load

## 5. Features / Benefits
- **Grid or card layout** (3–6 items)
- Each card: icon + bold title + 1–2 sentence benefit (not feature spec)
- Lead with the outcome for the user, not the technical detail

## 6. Social Proof
- Star count badge if available
- Testimonial placeholders or trust indicators
- "Used by X developers" or similar credibility line

## 7. Final CTA
- **Headline:** Urgency or aspiration-driven
- **CTA Button:** Same or complementary to hero CTA
- Optional: secondary link ("View Documentation", "See Pricing")

## 8. Footer
- Navigation links, copyright, social icons
- Keep minimal and clean
copyGuidelines:
# Copy Guidelines
- Lead with benefits, not features — answer "so what?" for every claim
- Use specific numbers when available (stars, download count, performance stats)
- Match the tone to the design preset (playful for casual, authoritative for enterprise)
- CTAs should be first-person: "Start My Project" not "Start Your Project"
- Keep paragraphs to 2–3 sentences max for scannability
- Use power words: effortless, instant, seamless, powerful, lightweight
- Headings should be benefit-driven, not label-driven ("Ship Faster" not "Speed")
technicalConstraints:
# Technical Constraints
- Mobile-first responsive design with breakpoints at 375px, 768px, and 1280px
- Semantic HTML5 elements (header, nav, main, section, footer)
- WCAG AA contrast ratios for all text
- Smooth scroll behavior for anchor navigation
- Subtle entrance animations (fade-in, slide-up) using CSS only — no heavy JS animation libraries
- Fast loading: inline critical CSS, minimize external requests
- All sections must have proper id attributes for navigation
inspirationSites:
# Inspiration Sites
Screenshots of these websites are attached as images. Study their visual design carefully — color palettes, typography, spacing, layout patterns, animations, and overall aesthetic. Match the look and feel of these reference designs as closely as possible.
inspirationSitesNoImages: # Inspiration Sites (match the look and feel of these)
readmeIntel: # README Intelligence (mapped to landing page sections)
geminiOutputFormat:
# Output Format
Respond with ONLY a JSON object where keys are file paths and values are file contents.
Example: {"index.html": "<!DOCTYPE html>...", "style.css": "body { ... }"}
Make the page fully self-contained. Use CDN links for any external libraries.
Include responsive design. Make it look polished and professional.
claudeSystemPrefix: You are building a landing page. Write all files into the current directory ({{outputDir}}).
claudeOutputFormat:
# Output
Write the landing page files directly into the current working directory.
Start with index.html as the entry point.
Make the page fully self-contained with an index.html entry point.
Use CDN links for any external libraries.
Include responsive design. Make it look polished and professional.
